reasoning: The 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship was filmed in early August 2025 and is set to broadcast in the fall. The base rate for any single player winning a 64-player heads-up tournament is around 1.5%. Reliable reporting from PokerScout in October 2025 indicates that the results of the tournament were leaked shortly after filming concluded, with Sam Soverel emerging as the presumed winner. According to the article, Soverel's Polymarket probability spiked to over 95% and remained above 92%, while Marius Gierse was not among the very few players trading above 1%. Given the strong evidence of a leaked outcome pointing to another player, the probability of Gierse winning is incredibly low and consistent with the market estimate of 2%.
